summary: Crystal‑clear 1977 soundboard capture of a rollicking show featuring a smoking Lazy Lightnin' > Supplication jam, stellar Wharf Rat and a fluid Playing‑in‑the‑Band sandwich.
review: The Miller tape delivers killer sound – crisp, well‑balanced board mix that lets every instrument shine. Musically the night is a high‑energy affair with a trippy Lazy Lightnin'→Supplication sequence, a fiery Wharf Rat, a beautifully executed Playing‑in‑the‑Band sandwich and standout moments from Peggy‑O, Samson and the first‑set classics. Overall reviewers rave about both the performance and the audio quality.

ai_rating.rationale: Six detailed reviews are uniformly enthusiastic (average 4.7★), praising both the superb soundboard recording and the exceptional playing, with only minor mixed comments.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Multiple reviewers call the audio "killer" and "amazing", noting clear instrument separation and no significant drop‑outs in this particular file.

show_quality.setlist_flow: High‑octane first set with a driving Deal closer, followed by an explosive second set highlighted by the Lazy Lightnin'→Supplication jam and a seamless Playing‑in‑the‑Band sandwich; encore (Johnny B. Goode) is generally liked despite one reviewer’s critique.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for his fiery solo work on Supplication and Me & My Uncle, with reviewers noting his “diffident” start that erupts into a full‑blown tear.
band_member_comments.Phil: Mentioned in passing as part of the tight rhythm section; no specific performance critique beyond general praise.
band_member_comments.Bob: Highlighted for his vocal leads (e.g., Me & My Uncle) and quirky stage banter, with reviewers noting his direction on tempo choices.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is noted for adding a harpsichord texture on Peggy‑O and generally contributing solid keyboard work throughout the show.
band_member_comments.Drums: Mickey’s call‑outs (e.g., asking for Bertha) are mentioned; no explicit criticism, implying steady drumming.
